Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Talbco Development Corporation And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Joshua Maltzer, Esq. 415-956-2828 Rogers Joseph O'Donnell for Plaintiff.
Alexander Moore, Esq. 510-859-8017 for Defendant.

ARGUED. Defendant Talbco contests the entire tentative ruling. Mr. Moore argues. Mr. Maltzer argues. The Court shares its comments. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF 123 CORBETT, LP's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Talbco Development Corporation And For Monetary Sanctions.

Taken Under submission.

A proposed order is submitted for the court's review.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Talbco Development Corporation And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-23-2024 Continued To Jan-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances made. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF 123 CORBETT, LP's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Talbco Development Corporation And For Monetary Sanctions.

Continued to January 23, 2025. Per SF Local Rule 2.7B, the parties are ordered to provide courtesy copies of their filings immediately with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date.

Judge: Victor M. Hwang; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not reported.(302/VMH)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Karin N. Kuemerle
Off Calendar Oct-24-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JOSHUA MALTZER, Esq. of ROGERS JOSEPH O'DONNELL (tel: 415-956-2828) for Plaintiff.
Atty. KARIN KUEMERLE, Esq. of KAHANA & FELD LLP (tel: 510-859-8017) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT TALBCO DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint.

Matter discussed. The Court notes the matter is not contested. Counsel Karin Kuemerle states the defendant would like to contest the tentative ruling, but concedes that the defendant failed to notify the Court of their intention to contest the tentative ruling. Upon conclusion, the Court does not allow counsel to present oral argument, and adopts its tentative ruling, as the matter was not contested in a timely fashion.

Defendant Talbco Development Corp.'s motion for leave to file cross-complaint is denied.

In this construction defect case, Talbco was general contractor for renovations of plaintiff's building. Talbco had at least eight subcontractors. In 2021, plaintiff sent defect reports to Talbco - reports that no doubt alerted Talbco to claims against its subcontractors. (Opp. 1:12-19.) In its December 29, 2023 answer, Talbco asserted that other parties (e.g., its subcontractors) were responsible for damage. (Id. at 2:3-10.) On January 8, plaintiff's counsel urged Talbco to cross-claim against the subcontractors immediately so trial would not be delayed. (Maltzer Dec. 1:4-11.) However, Talbco waited until now - four months before the February 24 trial date - to seek leave to cross-claim against the eight subcontractors. Were leave granted, discovery would likely push trial deep into 2025 or 2026.

Liberal amendment of pleadings is common in California, but a court may exercise its discretion to deny where the moving party has been dilatory. (Crocker Nat. Bank v. Emerald (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 852, 864.) This is such a case. Talbco's memorandum offers no explanation for its foot-dragging. Moreover, Talbco concedes it can bring an indemnity action against the subcontractors should plaintiff defeat it at trial. (Memo. 5:16-17.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
